sections.paragraph:
- Original: Φοίβη

- Transliteration: Phoibe

- Phonetic: foy'-bay

- Definition: Phoebe = "radiant"

1. a deaconess of the church at Cenchrea, near Corinth

- Origin: from phoibos (bright, probably akin to the base of G5457)

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech: Noun Feminine

- Strong's: Feminine of Φοῖβος Phoibos (bright; probably akin to the base of G5457); Phaebe a Christian woman: - Phebe.
